node.text:
Known as the “Patagonia of the North”, Tombstone Territorial Park is 2,200 squared kilometers of pristine wilderness with picturesque rugged peaks, permafrost landforms, and a wide array of unique flora and fauna.  ⁣
There are plenty of hiking trails and campsites - from easy beginner trails like the North Klondike River trail or Goldensides to back country overnight trips like Grizzly, Talus, or Divide Lake. ⁣
One of the best times to visit is during late summer when the tundra vegetation paints the landscape in vivid crimson, gold and red. Would you go explore this vast wilderness?⁣
⁣
